utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: next patient is sophia jackson , mrnr472348 . she's a 57 year old female who is here for a surgical consult . her dermatologist referred her . she biopsied a 0.7 millimeter lesion which was located on right inferior back . pathology came back as melanoma .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: mrs. jackson , it's good to meet you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: likewise , wish it were under better circumstances .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , i hear your dermatologist sent you to me 'cause she found a melanoma ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , that's what the biopsy said .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and when did you first notice the spot ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: my mom noticed it when i was visiting her last month .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i see . and so you went to the dermatologist on april 10th to get it checked out , right ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , i wanted to be extra cautious because skin cancer does run in my family .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: well i'm really glad you took it seriously and got it checked . who in your family has had skin cancer , and do you know if it was melanoma or was it basal cell or squamous cell ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: my mom and her sister , i think they both had melanoma .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . do you have any other types of cancer in the family , like breast or ovarian ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: my grandfather had pancreatic cancer .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and was that your mom or dad's father ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mother's .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and , um , have you personally had any skin spots in the past that you got checked out and they were cancerous or precancerous ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , this was the first time i've been to a dermatologist . um , but my primary care doctor looks over all of my moles every year at my physical and has n't said , um , he's concerned about any of 'em before .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good- good . uh , let's go over your medical history from your chart . i have that you're not taking any medications and do n't have any health problems listed , but that you're allergic to augmentin , is that right ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , that's correct .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and for social history can you tell me what you do for work ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i own an auto repair shop .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and have you ever been a smoker ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i still smoke from time to time . i started that awful habit in my teens and it's hard to break , but i'm trying .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm glad you're trying to quit . uh , what about your surgical history , have you had any surgeries ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i had gall bladder and appendix .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , great , we can get your chart up to date now , thank you . and other than the melanoma , how has your health been , any unintentional weight changes , headaches , fatigue , nausea , vomiting , vision changes ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , i've been feelin' great .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good . well let me take a look at your back here where they did the biopsy if you do n't mind .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , i'm gon na describe it in medical jargon what i'm seeing here , so that the recording can capture it , but you and i are gon na go over it together in just a moment , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , that's fine .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , so on the right inferior back there's a one centimeter shave biopsy site , including all of the dermis with no residual pigmentation . there's no intrinsic or satellite lesions , no other suspicious moles , no axillary , cervical , or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy . there is a soft lymph node in the right groin , but it's nontender , otherwise normal exam .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , you can sit up . um , so what i was saying there is that i see your biopsy site , but i do n't see any other s- , um , skin lumps or bumps that look suspicious . uh , i also felt your lymph nodes to see if any of them felt abnormal . there is one in the right groin that felt slightly abnormal . it's very likely nothing , but i do want you to have an ultrasound of that area to confirm it's nothing , um , and , you know , make sure it's nothing that we need to worry about . uh , the reason we're being extra cautious is that melanoma can very rarely metastasize to the lymph nodes . the ultrasound can tell us if we need to look into this further .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , i should n't worry too much then ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no , i have a low suspicion that it will show anything .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so assuming that the ultrasound is normal , the treatment for you melanoma is to cut out the area where the lesion was . with lesions that are 0.7 millimeters or less , um , and that's what we recommend , and yours was exactly 0.7 millimeters . if it were any bigger , we would have had to do a more complex surgery . but what i recommend for you is what we call a wide local incision , excuse me , excision , meaning that i will make a long incision and then cut out an area a bit wider than your current biopsy site . the incision is long because that's what allows me to close the skin nicely . you'll have a fairly long scar from the incision .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , that is fine with me , i ca n't see back there anyways .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , your wife can tell you what it looks like and she may need to help care for the incision at it , as it heals . um , but since we're , we are n't doing the more complex surgery , i actually do n't need to see you back unless you want to check in with me or have any problems . however , it is very important that you continue to follow up with your dermatologist regularly so she can monitor you . uh , your dermatologist will check that this one does n't come back , but she'll also check for other lesions that look suspicious . uh , unfortunately , since you've had one melanoma , you're at a higher risk of developing another one somewhere else .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , she did say she wants to see me back .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good , and i'm sure she's already told you , but it's very important that you apply sunscreen anytime and anywhere that your skin is exposed to sunlight .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , she definitely went over that , um , several times with me .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good . other than that , i think that's all for me . um , we'll get you set up for the ultrasound , the procedure . do you have any questions for me ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , no i ca n't think of any at this time .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , my nurse will be in to get you scheduled , so sit tight . it was very good to meet you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: thank you , nice to meet you as well .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: please add the following pathology r- , to results . a pathology , shave of right inferior back , malignant melanoma , invasive , superficial spreading . histology , superficial spreading . clark level 4 , breslow thickness 0.7 millimeters , radial growth phase present , vertical growth phase not identified . mitotic features , less than one millimeter squared . ulceration not identified , progression not identified , lymphatic invasion not identified , perineural invasion not identified , microscopic satellitosis not identified . infiltrating , uh , lymphocytes , breast . um , melanocytic nevus not identified . predominant cytology epithelioid , peripheral margin positive , deep margin , uh , negative , stage 1 . also note that i reviewed the dermatologist's photo of the lesion which showed an asymmetric black and brown nevus with central a melanotic component and irregular border .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: for assessment and plan , the patient presents today with newly diagnosed melanoma . biopsy revealed an intermediate thickness melanoma . on examination today , there is right inguinal lymph node with slightly atypical consistency . i recommended an ultrasound to rule out metastatic disease . if the ultrasound is normal , the patient is a candidate for wide local excision with a one to two centimeter margin .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: primary closure should be possible , but skin graft closure may be needed . the relationship between tumor histology and prognosis and treatment was carefully reviewed . the need for follow-up , according to the national comprehensive cancer network guidelines , was reviewed . we also reviewed the principles of sun avoidance , skin self-examination , and the abcdes of mole surveillance .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: after discussing the procedure , risk and expected outcomes , and possible complications , questions were answered and the patient expressed understanding and did choose to proceed .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 57-year-old female presenting for a surgical consult for melanoma of the right inferior back.
Melanoma
The doctor asks when the patient first noticed the lesion.
The patient's mother noticed the lesion last month.
The patient had a biopsy performed by a dermatologist on 04/10.
The doctor asks about the family history of skin cancer.
The patient's mother and maternal aunt have a history of melanoma.
The doctor asks about family history of breast or ovarian cancer.
The patient's maternal grandfather had pancreatic cancer.
The doctor asks if the patient has a personal history of suspicious skin lesions.
The patient denies a personal history of atypical nevi and reports annual mole checks by her primary care provider.
The patient has no current medications and an allergy to Augmentin.
The doctor asks about the patient's occupation.
The patient owns an auto repair shop.
The doctor asks about the patient's smoking history.
The patient is a current smoker since her teens and is trying to quit.
The doctor asks about the patient's surgical history.
The patient has a surgical history of cholecystectomy and appendectomy.
The doctor asks about systemic symptoms including weight changes, headaches, fatigue, nausea, vomiting, or vision changes.
The patient denies systemic symptoms.
Physical exam shows a 1 cm shave biopsy site on the right inferior back, no lymphadenopathy, and a nontender soft lymph node in the right groin.
The doctor orders an ultrasound of the right groin to rule out metastatic disease.
The patient asks if she should be worried about the lymph node.
The doctor expresses low suspicion of malignancy regarding the lymph node.
The doctor recommends a wide local excision for the 0.7 mm melanoma.
The patient is instructed to follow up regularly with her dermatologist for skin surveillance.
The doctor reinforces the importance of sun avoidance and sunscreen use.
The doctor asks if the patient has any questions.
Pathology confirms invasive superficial spreading melanoma, 0.7 mm Breslow thickness, Clark level 4, with positive peripheral margins.
The plan includes an ultrasound for the right inguinal lymph node and a wide local excision with 1-2 cm margins if the ultrasound is normal.
The doctor reviewed NCCN guidelines, sun avoidance, skin self-examination, and ABCDE mole surveillance with the patient.
